common.status_current: Fifth-semester student at Ostfalia University & Software / DevOps Engineer at GGU-Software
common.portrait_alt: Generative code portrait of Eslam Metawie

home.intro_text_1: I believe that high-quality software engineering doesn't stop at compiling code. It extends all the way to how applications are tested, packaged, deployed, updated, and monitored in production. My work revolves around making release cycles reliable and repeatable, removing manual barriers, and maintaining robust codebase standards.
home.intro_text_2: Through my studies and GGU-Software work, I balance core academic computer science with immediate hands-on engineering challenges. Whether automating Windows desktop installer packaging or deploying full-stack web platforms, I focus on software system reliability.
home.focus_sw_desc: Creating type-safe backends, RESTful interfaces, and clean responsive frontends with React and Java/Spring Boot.
home.focus_devops_desc: Building automated build, test, and release flows using GitHub Actions, Tekton, and custom scripting.
home.focus_infra_desc: Managing Docker-based microservices, Azure virtual environments, and monitoring system statuses.
home.projects_sub: A selection of SaaS platforms, responsive interfaces, and pipeline configurations.
home.cta_github_desc: Many of my configurations, academic tasks, and UI experiments are open source on GitHub. Take a look at the code quality and structures.
home.cta_linkedin_desc: Let's connect on LinkedIn. I am always open to exchanging ideas about software architecture, DevOps pipelines, and digital products.
home.pipeline_desc: Release cycles should never rely on memory or repetitive manual sequences. I build pipelines that compile code, execute checks, package installers, and trigger staging updates in a structured, observable path.
home.banner_title: Need reliable software pipelines or custom development?
home.banner_desc: Let's build a repeatable and robust release infrastructure. Open to exchange ideas and collaborate.

pipeline.desc_code: Clean code practices, Git, Spring Boot & React development
pipeline.desc_build: Automated compiler tasks, project packaging, MSBuild & Gradle
pipeline.desc_test: JUnit, automated integration tests, coverage verification
pipeline.desc_package: Docker container creations & Chocolatey package publishing
pipeline.desc_deploy: Azure virtual servers, environment monitoring, IIS/Kubernetes updates
pipeline.hover_hint: Hover over a node to inspect the automation lifecycle

about.page_subtitle: Profile, career path, and technical focus at a glance.
about.intro_p1: I am Eslam Metawie, a Computer Science student in my 5th semester at Ostfalia University of Applied Sciences and a Working Student at GGU Software in Braunschweig. My focus is Software Engineering and DevOps: I build reliable software and automate the paths it takes to be tested, packaged, and delivered.
about.summary_p1: My studies and professional work run in parallel: at university I deepen the foundations of computer science, while at GGU Software I work on real release and automation processes every day. This combination shapes how I work – I think about software from architecture all the way to delivery.
about.summary_p2: In the DevOps space, I automate build, test, and deployment workflows and value maintainable, clearly structured solutions. Alongside that, I build my own projects – from full-stack web applications to pipeline configurations – and continuously expand my technical skills.
about.ggu_desc: On the DevOps team, I support the release processes of the company's desktop applications – from the pipeline all the way to customer delivery.
about.ggu_task_1: Building and maintaining Jenkins pipelines for automated release processes of the desktop applications.
about.ggu_task_2: Packaging software and reliably delivering updates to customers via the Chocolatey package manager.
about.ggu_task_3: Running CI builds including automated tests, artifact creation, and version management.
about.ggu_task_4: Version control, branch management, and collaboration within the DevOps team using Git and GitHub.
about.ggu_task_5: Provisioning development and test environments with Docker and Kubernetes.
about.ggu_task_6: Automating recurring tasks with shell scripts and Python, working closely with the development teams to improve deployment processes.
about.dos_desc: Contributed to the development of a web application for agile teams – from the first wireframes to finished frontend and backend features.
about.dos_task_1: Implemented frontend and backend features with React, Spring Boot, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
about.dos_task_2: Created wireframes in Figma to visualize customer requirements.
about.dos_task_3: Used CI/CD pipelines, Bitbucket, and Podman to automate the development process.
about.skbs_desc: Worked in data entry and maintenance with Microsoft Office and Outlook, including organizing and maintaining interdisciplinary scheduling lists for medical staff.
about.ostfalia_degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
about.ostfalia_desc: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science with a focus on Software Engineering.
about.mansoura_desc: Studied two semesters of Computer Science before transferring to Germany to continue my studies there.
about.interest_travel_desc: Discovering new places, cultures, and perspectives.
about.interest_fishing_desc: Patience and calm by the water – the best balance to screen time.
about.interest_astronomy_desc: Looking up at the night sky, preferably far from city lights.
about.philosophy_p1: Good software is not created in the editor alone. Clean architecture and maintainable code are the foundation – but the path to production matters just as much: automated builds, meaningful tests, and releases that can be repeated without manual steps.
about.philosophy_p2: That is why I see DevOps not as a role but as a way of working: development and operations share responsibility for software arriving reliably. And because tools and platforms evolve constantly, continuous learning is a fixed part of it for me.
about.principle_1_desc: Clear structures and code the next team member will actually enjoy reading.
about.principle_2_desc: Recurring workflows belong in pipelines, not in checklists.
about.principle_3_desc: Automated tests as the standard, not an afterthought.
about.principle_4_desc: Regularly exploring and deepening new tools and concepts.

projects.page_subtitle: Real digital products, corporate designs, and DevOps practice pipelines.
projects.more_projects_desc: For more utility tools, academic task submissions, and scripting challenges, check out my active repositories directly on GitHub.

darsio.name: Darsio
darsio.short: An all-in-one teaching-management platform for private teachers and learning centers.
darsio.desc: Darsio is a comprehensive learning and administrative management platform built to simplify workflows for private tutors and academies. It centralizes student profiles, class attendance sheets, payment states, assignments, and parent progress updates into a singular dashboard. Built with a scalable multi-tenant architecture and strict authentication protocols.
features: Interactive dashboards for teachers, students, and parents
Student attendance tracking and course material distributions
Assignments upload portal with automated grading tools
Payment status logs and reminder alerts
Token-based secure client access and multilingual translations
Multi-tenant data isolation and role-based access control (RBAC)

bedaya.name: BEDAYA
bedaya.short: A bilingual architecture and construction corporate website with fluid RTL/LTR design.
bedaya.desc: BEDAYA represents a responsive, modern corporate web showcase developed for BEDAYA Constructions & Development. The platform exhibits commercial and residential construction projects using a clean grid interface, supporting instant translation switching between English (LTR) and Arabic (RTL).
features: High-performance static hosting on Cloudflare Pages
Dynamic content translation switching without layout shifts
RTL-aware grid and typographic hierarchy for Arabic readers
Interactive architectural galleries showing project details
Accessibility-vetted layouts with optimized media files

cafe.name: Café Zeitlos
cafe.short: A clean hospitality website displaying menu lists, galleries, and visitor info.
cafe.desc: A frontend digital menu and gallery website tailored for Café Zeitlos, a local dining spot in Braunschweig, Germany. Designed to introduce the café's story, display dietary menus (featuring halal items), and present visitor location instructions in a modern responsive layout.
features: Fluid CSS grids and custom theme variables
Interactive, categorized food and beverage menus
Dynamic layout animations and visual hover states
SEO metadata optimization for local searches in Braunschweig
Multilingual ready structure

cicd.name: Intro to CI/CD Practice Code
cicd.short: An educational project exploring automated testing, Tekton, and container deployment workflows.
cicd.desc: A pipeline engineering sandbox created as a fork of IBM Developer Skills Network's Introduction to CI/CD course codebase. This repository was used to practice and execute cloud-native automation setups, configuring triggers, container image processes, and deployment tasks.
cicd.attribution: Forked from IBM Skills Network. Original course instruction by John Rofrano (Senior Technical Staff Member and DevOps Champion at IBM Research).
features: Configuring custom GitHub Actions pipelines with trigger hooks
Integrating Tekton CD catalog resources for modular pipelines
Automating unit test executions on code check-ins
Building container images and simulating Kubernetes target updates
Setting up testing dependencies inside Python web apps

privacy.body_data: This website operates primarily as a static showcase. It does not actively track cookies, collect analytics scripts, or gather personally identifiable information unless you explicitly fill out the Contact Form. Form submissions are processed securely and only used to respond to your specific inquiries.
privacy.body_contact: Information sent via the contact form (name, email, subject, message) is forwarded to the designated site email for communication purposes. This data is never shared with third parties or sold for commercial objectives.
privacy.body_rights: You maintain the right to ask for a copy of any personal data you have submitted to us, or request its deletion at any time by reaching out via the contact form.
